tldr: Headlines are dominated by the collapse of the brief ceasefire and resumed drone exchanges, with a secondary corruption/sanctions thread and a smaller political cluster on stalled peace diplomacy.

silences.note: One of two headlines ('Could influencers crash future EU summits?') is entirely off-topic; the on-topic Conversation headline covers Victory Day and Russia's position. No distinctive framing angle emerges at headline level.

War on the Rocks headline ('Sensationalism Doesn't Serve Society') is a media-criticism piece with no Ukraine framing; the second headline covers Russia's crowd-sourced defense industry, which is on-topic but niche.

bottom_line: The headline layer reads the truce expiry as the dominant event. The corruption/sanctions thread is the headline-level signature of accountability coverage that body text distributes across more outlets.
